options.text: Can you summarize the main productivity techniques from this article? I want to be able to reference them later.

Extract the key productivity techniques from this article and format them as 5 numbered bullet points with the technique name and a brief explanation of how to implement it. I need this for quick reference during my workday.

responses.response_weak: I would be happy to summarize an article for you, but I do not see any article content in your message. Could you please share the article text or a link so I can help?
responses.response_medium:
I would be glad to pull out the main productivity techniques for you! However, I do not see the article content in your message. Could you paste the article text so I can summarize the key techniques?

Once you share it, I will identify the core productivity methods discussed and present them in a way that is easy to reference later.
responses.response_strong:
I am ready to extract the key productivity techniques for you! I do not see the article content in your message yet, but once you share it, here is exactly what I will deliver:

**Format:**
1. **[Technique Name]** — One-sentence explanation of how to implement it in your workday
2. **[Technique Name]** — One-sentence explanation of how to implement it in your workday
3. *(and so on, up to 5 techniques)*

Each point will be concise enough to scan in under 30 seconds so you can reference it quickly between tasks. Go ahead and paste the article whenever you are ready.

feedback.what_happened: The weak prompt asks 'Summarize this article' — you'd mean 'pull out the useful parts in a format I can actually reference,' but the AI reads it as a wide-open request and doesn't know what kind of summary you need, how long it should be, or how you'll use it. The medium prompt adds that you want productivity techniques for later reference, so the AI knows the topic — but it's still guessing about format. The strong prompt specifies exactly 5 numbered bullet points, technique name in bold with implementation explanation, for quick workday reference — so the AI mirrors back that precise structure, confirming full alignment before work even begins.
feedback.clarification_response: I'd be happy to summarize an article for you! To make the summary as useful as possible, could you tell me: What do you want to pull out of it (key arguments, action items, techniques)? How will you use the summary (quick reference, sharing with someone, studying)? And do you have a preferred format (bullet points, paragraph, table)? Also, please paste the article text!
feedback.principle: Describing the exact format you want — number of items, structure of each item, level of detail — eliminates guesswork and ensures the output is immediately usable.
feedback.tip: Include a format description in your prompt: 'Give me 5 numbered bullet points with the name in bold and one sentence of explanation.' The AI will match your structure precisely, saving you from reformatting later.
